channel.title: Comments for page "A Great Idea, Probably..."
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Great Idea, Probably..."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you try to edit the page, you get to view the complete list of things the ideas can be generated from. From it, I determined that if one is exceptionally lucky, it might propose "A combustible lemon that burns your house down."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a gay dragon that is a firm believer in solipsism? Cliche, right? But guys, the twist is that it just wants someone to talk to. Would that work?''
I've harnessed this to it's potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"How about a psychic bell that grants wishes?"

*leans in closer*

Edit: I realize that this is, in fact, rather unoriginal. But who cares, I'm a sucker for cliches. Why do you think I'm on TV Tropes so much?

Edit 2: Check out this one!

"Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a chubby dragon that thinks humans are delicious? Cliche, right? Now, guys, the twist is that it thinks humans are the best thing ever. Something like that?"

WHAT!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"How about an intelligent computer virus that makes a mess? Cliche, right? But like, the twist is that it will kill anyone who makes fun of it."

Pretty sure this exact SCP was deleted a couple months ago lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a smart warrior that will save the world? Cliche, right? But it's not because, see, the twist is that it's a pilgrim on a sacred quest. It makes sense, right?"

Um…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least it's not just a half winded exposition from two years ago… Most of the tales I've read so far are incomplete/ and or uncomprehensable to me, but I'm fairly new to the whole SCP thing. I like the generator, and I almost s*** myself when I saw my screen name was the foundation scientist in the story. It fits so well LOL! The only thing I can see making it better is to add more to the tale, and increase the interactivity with the reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a smart CD that is maaaagic? Cliche, right? But like, the twist is that it's Satan's long-lost nephew. Would that work?''

Pretty sure we have like three of these, but it's still pretty funny.

Other results I found amusing in my sleep-deprived state:

''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about an astounding deer that is a firm believer in solipsism? Cliche, right? But guys, the twist is that it created other anomalies. Something like that?''

SATURN DEEEEEEEEER!

''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a sharp box of doughnuts that makes you physically attracted to sheep? Cliche, right? Wait, guys, so the twist, the twist is that it thinks it's a scientist. Got it, guys?''

I got that reference.

''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a chubby alien that everyone loves? Cliche, right? But, see, the twist is that all its friends are dead. You guys got that?''

A chubby alien. Everyone loves it, but as soon as you befriend it… you're dead. Spoopy.

''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about an ancient baseball bat that crucified Satan? Cliche, right? Now guys, the twist is that it's a pilgrim on a sacred quest. Something like that?''

I guess you could call this… *sunglasses* a bat outta hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thank you, I was trying to figure out what happened. In the first or second generator?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At this point, it's not showing anything for me, so in addition to being a pretty contentless addition to this website, it's not actually a functioning addition to this website. Testing in Firefox 48.0.2 (desktop browser), Firefox 48.0 for Android, Chrome 52.02743.98 for Android and Internet Explorer 11.0.9600.18015.

Aside from that, the code is pretty awful. If you want a cleaned up version, let me know.

Edit: your problem is a newline down where you've commented out your future additions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's mildly amusing once you realize what it's doing, but it's not substantial enough to be anything other than a gimmick that quickly wears thin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm adding more to the code gradually, as I'm trying to get multiple variables going on with a few additions each time so that I don't screw things up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adding "cliché, right" to the results isn't really fixing anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This could be used in so many more SCP's.

Ehhhh. Sometimes the username-generator coding backfires and breaks suspension of disbelief, and people downvote for the narm. Context is key! It's a tool, but not necessary a multipurpose one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I won't downvote it because I think it's a clever piece of code… but there's already a forum post that links to the idea generator I assume you're using. As it stands, this is just a bit of window dressing to disguise a huge amount of redundancy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a decent idea generator and should probably be on a sandbox somewhere.

It's debatable whether or not it should go on the mainsite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I can appreciate the technical skill needed to create a generator such as this, there is no intrinsic /writing/ ability required to do so. This is not a piece of creative writing, this is a piece of code wrapped in a flimsy shell of only a few paragraphs.

Maybe it's just me, but I come to a creative writing site with the expectation of finding creative writing. If I want code tidbits, I'll go to StackExchange.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or, what may be my favorite use of it: SCP-1893.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a horny French Horn is secretly a detective But, see, the twist is that it's really a soviet spy.

>_<

This can sure … uh .. make some interesting results

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is already used in quite a few, most notably this one.

item.title: Re: hey my name is 8lu3
item.content:encoded: oh but i would be funny if it was me, in real life i act weirdly like scp2020

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was freaked out for a moment why I was in an SCP, but looked into the document. An amazing idea, creepy as well. This could be used in so many more SCP's. It would be amazing to do this sought of thing with an SCP that could predict names, or something that wrote the name and details of a person without knowing beforehand.

item.title: Re: hey my name is 8lu3
item.content:encoded:
It's a bit of coding trickey to insert a reader's username.

Pretty much have the same issue as everyone else here. Not much in the way of a story for this tale (and 2020 appears in name only), and there's not much I can get out of the generator as-is.

item.title: hey my name is 8lu3
item.content:encoded: i just joined two days, why am i in a tale already

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find myself agreeing with the stance that it's a neat idea but a little undercooked. There's just not much to it story-wise… I was hoping for more. A random idea generator is a cool thing to work into a tale, but it's too barebones here.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might work on some ways to make things more varied, I might have to rework a bit of the code though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can appreciate the existence of this, but I'm agreeing with most of those above that it could have a bit more and some tweaking to make it both an entertaining work and a useful ideas generator. Without that, it's not much different from existing idea generators off-site.

Provisional novote for now, liable to change based on how you choose to improve or not improve this.

EDIT: Having generated a bunch more ideas, this does also suffer from not having enough of the predicate statements. There's only so much difference between a creepy emu that is secretly the grim reaper and a non-anomalous tree that is secretly the grim reaper. The problem is that the predicate is, for the most part, the bit that might allow writers to develop more of a story off the idea, since it defines the main "point" of the idea and not just a physical description.

As for how to fix that, maybe making the predicate section consist of multiple randomized parts? It'd result in more duds, but it'd also have more of a possibility to generate something useful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. I don't feel like this really adds anything to the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I'm biased here, since SCP-2020 is my baby. It's a cute framing device, but the generator itself could do a lot more, so no vote.

Immediate improvements, beyond expanding the scope of the things being generated, are finding a way to not make that text Times New Roman (what an ugly font, and more importantly it sticks out like a sore thumb) and touching up 2020's dialogue.

When I wrote 2020's monologue, I tried to get across that it is easily distracted, energetic, has no filter, and is stuck in 'elevator pitch' mode. It overuses the words 'guys', 'wait', 'twist', and 'but', and obviously it uses "Cliche, right?". Basically it's pretty distinctive. You're certainly entitled to write your own take on 2020 that better suits your style and/or purposes, but I can tell you that this isn't the 2020 that people enjoyed in the original article. Up to you which of those, if any, to implement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with the above comments asking for a bit more of an expansion or something, but honestly when I first clicked on the generator I was pretty amazed. Good job. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a thing in itself, it's a lolrandom thing generator, and after a while "hey random thing" just isn't amusing anymore. Plus we've already got the madlibs -J that covers something of the same ground.

As a commentary on the site, this seems like it would be a "bad articles sure are bad" entry if it were more coherent, which is like shooting fish in a barrel. It also completely misses the point of the original article it's referencing. This isn't even a cliche generator, since it's referencing one site specific cliche (thing what does a thing) exclusively.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
''Oh! I got an idea for something we could write about. How about a frozen professor that causes diabetes?''

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta agree with Decibelles to be honest. It feels like you could do more with this. I like the generator though.

It's gotta be a no vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanna support the idea but it surely can't hurt to have like, more of a story and a bit more interactivity with the generator (maybe more dialog between the main character and 2020, with the generator idea popping up more than once). Too bare-bones for me to support.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think you understand the point of this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to ChaoSera for helping me with the JavaScript code.

The story bit's short, yes, but the focus of this is really on the generator itself, so I don't want to go burying it in exposition.

basically, this is an XX that does Y generator, designed as a way to play around with not-so-great ideas, sort-of-weird ideas, and hasn't-that-been-done-already? ideas. It might make you laugh, it might cure writer's block. Who knows?

Want to suggest additions?

you can suggest nouns, adjectives, things-that-it-does, and twists.

I especially need twists so if anyone has any idea that would be great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please complete this.
